evals.input: How do I initialize a GORM database with proper connection pooling and a startup health check?
expected_concepts: Call db.DB() to get the underlying *sql.DB for pool configuration
SetMaxOpenConns caps concurrent connections; default is unlimited
SetMaxIdleConns keeps connections alive to avoid reconnect overhead
SetConnMaxLifetime recycles connections before server-side timeout
PingContext with a short timeout verifies DB is reachable before serving traffic

evals.input: How do I implement a transaction that spans multiple store operations in a layered Go app?
expected_concepts: TX(ctx, fn) wraps fn in a GORM transaction and injects tx into context via transactionKey
DB(ctx) extracts tx from context automatically — store methods stay transaction-unaware
Biz layer calls store.TX(ctx, func(ctx) error { ... }) to start a transaction
All store.DB(ctx) calls inside fn automatically use the same tx
Transaction commits on nil return, rolls back on error

evals.input: How should I structure the IStore interface and its datastore implementation in a Go project?
expected_concepts: IStore aggregates all sub-store accessors (User(), Post()) plus DB() and TX() methods
datastore holds *gorm.DB as core field; NewStore uses sync.Once for singleton initialization
Wire ProviderSet binds *datastore to IStore interface for dependency injection
DB(ctx) accepts variadic where.Where for composable filtering and pagination
Expose IStore in biz constructors, not *datastore directly

should_not_trigger.input: How do I run database migrations in a Go project using golang-migrate?
should_not_trigger.reason: Migration tooling is separate from DB client wrapping — out of scope for this skill

should_not_trigger.input: How do I handle errors returned from database operations in my store layer?
should_not_trigger.reason: golang-error-handling covers errno sentinel translation and layered error propagation
